fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {C} be a wild category; a quasi-idempotent on an object A: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} } is defined to be a pre-idempotent { \mathopen {} \left ( f, \phi \right ) \mathclose {}} on A together with an identification \chi between f; \phi : f; { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} = f;f and the following composite identification:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: f; { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { assoc } _{ \mathcal {C} } \, f \, f \, f} { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} ;f \xrightarrow { \phi ;f} f;f

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The definitions here are adapted from Shulman from the setting of intensional type theory to an arbitrary wild category.

Let \mathcal {C} be a wild category; a pre-idempotent on an object A: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} } is defined to be a morphism f: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , A \right ) \mathclose {}} together with an identification \phi : f;f = f.

Let \mathcal {C} be a wild category; a quasi-idempotent on an object A: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} } is defined to be a pre-idempotent { \mathopen {} \left ( f, \phi \right ) \mathclose {}} on A together with an identification \chi between f; \phi : f; { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} = f;f and the following composite identification:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: f; { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { assoc } _{ \mathcal {C} } \, f \, f \, f} { \mathopen {} \left ( f;f \right ) \mathclose {}} ;f \xrightarrow { \phi ;f} f;f

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {C} be a wild category; a pre-idempotent on an object A: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} } is defined to be a morphism f: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , A \right ) \mathclose {}} together with an identification \phi : f;f = f.
A wild category is given by the following data:

fr:ol.fr:li: for each A,B: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} }, a family of types \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , B \right ) \mathclose {}};
for each A: \mathbf {ob} _{ \mathcal {C} }, an element 1 _{ A } : \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , A \right ) \mathclose {}};

for each f: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , B \right ) \mathclose {}} and g: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( B , C \right ) \mathclose {}}, an element { \mathopen {} \left ( f;g \right ) \mathclose {}} : \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , C \right ) \mathclose {}}

for each f: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , B \right ) \mathclose {}}, a identification \mathsf { lunit } _{ \mathcal {C} } f : { \mathopen {} \left ( 1 _{ A } ;f \right ) \mathclose {}} = f and a identification \mathsf { runit } _{ \mathcal {C} } f : { \mathopen {} \left ( f; 1 _{ B } \right ) \mathclose {}} =f;

for each f: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( A , B \right ) \mathclose {}} and g: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( B , C \right ) \mathclose {}} and h: \mathbf {hom} _{ \mathcal {C} } { \mathopen {} \left ( C , D \right ) \mathclose {}}, a identification \mathsf { assoc } _{ \mathcal {C} } \, f \, g \, h : f; { \mathopen {} \left ( g;h \right ) \mathclose {}} = { \mathopen {} \left ( f;g \right ) \mathclose {}} ;h.
